symbols.name: StandardReelBuilderFactory
symbols.detail: [USED] Runtime-imported by 1 file: src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Implementation correctly satisfies the abstract contract: iterates 0..reelCount-1 and delegates each reel to spinReel(i). The ignored _rowCount is an explicitly documented and accepted design consequence (ADR-002). No logic errors or type mismatches visible. Any RNG-correctness concerns live in spinReel (src/reels.ts), not here. | [OVER] A four-line for-loop wrapped in a class that extends an abstract base with 0 importers. With only 1 runtime importer and no consumer ever referencing the abstract type, the class hierarchy delivers no polymorphism in practice. ADR-002 justifies the factory pattern for future test-double scenarios, but since AbstractReelBuilderFactory itself has 0 importers those scenarios have not been acted upon. A standalone function `buildReels(reelCount: number): Symbol[][]` would be equally extensible and easier to swap in tests via module mocking, without the class indirection ADR-002 itself flags as a negative consequence.

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 9/10 (1 FAIL, 1 WARN, 15 PASS)

rules.detail: tsconfig is not included in the provided context, but all parameters and return types are explicitly annotated throughout the file, which is consistent with strict mode being enabled. No implicit-any patterns are visible.
No explicit or implicit `any` types present. All types are fully annotated (`number`, `Symbol[][]`).
No type assertions or union types exist in this file. The abstract class pattern cleanly models the variant hierarchy without any unsafe narrowing.
No utility types are needed in this file. The types expressed (`Symbol[][]`) are straightforwardly composed and do not benefit from Pick/Omit/Record etc.

The return type of `buildReels` is `Symbol[][]` (mutable). Callers could accidentally mutate the reel grid. Prefer `readonly Symbol[][]` or `ReadonlyArray<ReadonlyArray<Symbol>>` to enforce immutability at the boundary, which is especially important given the gambling domain where grid integrity after construction is a correctness invariant.
The file uses abstract classes, which are appropriate here for expressing a constructable factory contract. The `import type` syntax is correctly used for the `Symbol` type import.
File is 16 lines, well within the 300-line limit.
The unused `rowCount` parameter is correctly prefixed with `_` (`_rowCount`), satisfying the `no-unused-vars` rule. No other lint issues are visible.

Both exported classes (`AbstractReelBuilderFactory` and `StandardReelBuilderFactory`) lack JSDoc documentation. The abstract class in particular defines the public contract used by the engine and test doubles, and should document the semantics of `reelCount` and `rowCount` (especially since `rowCount` is intentionally ignored per ADR-002, which is non-obvious to consumers).
Uses `.js` ESM-compatible extensions for imports, `import type` for type-only imports, and abstract class syntax. No deprecated APIs used.

Imports are cleanly grouped: type import first, then value import. Both resolve to peer modules within `src/` as expected by the project structure. No side-effect imports or circular dependency risk apparent.
No asynchronous code in this file. `buildReels` is synchronous and delegates to `spinReel`, which is also synchronous. No error handling surface exists here.

Slot-machine domain inferred from project vocabulary (reels, jackpot, freespin, wild, paytable). Per instruction 11, RNG safety concerns belong to `src/reels.ts` and `src/rng.ts` (where the actual RNG is defined), not to this factory which merely delegates to `spinReel(i)`. No hardcoded secrets, eval, or injection vectors exist in this file.
Simple O(reelCount) loop with no nested iterations, no redundant allocations, and no synchronous I/O. No performance concerns.

Per ADR-002 (`.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md`), testability is achieved by substituting the entire factory with a test double rather than by injecting `spinReel`. This is the documented and intentional design decision. The abstract class contract makes test doubles trivially easy to author.

No clear opportunities for `satisfies`, const type parameters, or `using` declarations in this file. The class hierarchy already provides the type safety that `satisfies` would offer. The simplicity of the file makes adoption of newer features a non-issue.

This is a game-engine utility file. The factory pattern is the correct architectural choice for a reel-construction abstraction. The `_rowCount` unused parameter is acknowledged and documented in ADR-002 as an accepted trade-off. No React, API, or other context-specific anti-patterns apply.

suggestions.description: Add JSDoc to both exported classes to document the contract, especially the semantics of the unused `rowCount` parameter which is non-obvious to consumers.
Return a readonly type from `buildReels` to prevent callers from accidentally mutating the generated reel grid, which is important for grid integrity in the gambling engine.
